security_issues.finding: Service URLs are hardcoded (e.g., 'http://payment-service:8084/api/v1/payments'). While not a secret, these should be externalized to configuration.
security_issues.recommendation: Move service URLs to application.yml or environment variables. Use Spring Cloud Config or Kubernetes ConfigMaps.
security_issues.cwe: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials (adjacent risk)

security_issues.finding: Cart item fields (productId, price, quantity) are cast from Map<String, Object> without null checks. Malformed cart data could cause NullPointerException.
security_issues.recommendation: Add null checks before casting. Validate each field. Consider using typed DTOs instead of raw Maps.

security_issues.finding: RuntimeException wraps the original exception which may contain internal service details. This could leak sensitive information in error responses.
security_issues.recommendation: Wrap in custom OrderCreationException without exposing inner exception message to callers.

security_issues.finding: Inter-service REST calls do not include authentication headers. In production, services should validate tokens even for internal calls.
security_issues.recommendation: Implement service-to-service authentication (mTLS or internal JWT tokens).

missing_null_checks.issue: cart.get('items') could return null if cart structure is malformed
missing_null_checks.fix: Add: if (cart == null || !cart.containsKey('items')) throw new IllegalStateException('Invalid cart data')

missing_null_checks.code: BigDecimal discount = cart.get("discount") != null ? new BigDecimal(...) : BigDecimal.ZERO;
missing_null_checks.issue: cart.get('discount').toString() could fail if value is not a valid number
missing_null_checks.fix: Wrap in try-catch or use NumberFormatException-safe parsing

missing_null_checks.issue: Product could be null but the null check only logs warning for non-IllegalStateException
missing_null_checks.fix: Add explicit null check: if (product == null) throw new ProductNotFoundException(...)

missing_null_checks.issue: If order.getItems() returns null (unlikely but possible with lazy loading issues), NPE

complexity_analysis.recommendation: Refactor createOrder into smaller methods: validateCart(), buildOrderItems(), calculateTotals(), processPayment(). Target CC < 10 per method.

refactor_suggestions.title: Extract Order Creation Steps
refactor_suggestions.current: createOrder() handles cart fetch, validation, item mapping, total calculation, payment, and cart clearing in one method (170+ lines, CC=17)
refactor_suggestions.proposed: Split into: fetchAndValidateCart(), mapCartToOrderItems(), calculateOrderTotals(), initiateAndTrackPayment(), postOrderCleanup()
refactor_suggestions.benefit: Reduces complexity, improves testability, enables independent retry logic per step

refactor_suggestions.title: Replace Raw Maps with Typed DTOs
refactor_suggestions.current: Inter-service responses are deserialized as Map<String, Object> with unsafe casts
refactor_suggestions.proposed: Create CartResponse, ProductResponse DTOs and use RestTemplate.getForObject(url, CartResponse.class)
refactor_suggestions.benefit: Type safety, compile-time checks, eliminates ClassCastException risks

refactor_suggestions.title: Implement Saga Pattern for Distributed Transaction
refactor_suggestions.current: Order creation, payment, and inventory update are not atomically linked. Payment failure leaves order in inconsistent state.
refactor_suggestions.proposed: Implement choreography-based saga: Order → Payment → Inventory. Each step has compensating transaction.
refactor_suggestions.benefit: Data consistency across services, automatic rollback on failure

refactor_suggestions.title: Add Circuit Breaker Pattern
refactor_suggestions.current: REST calls to services have simple try-catch with no circuit breaker. Failed services cause cascading timeouts.
refactor_suggestions.proposed: Add Resilience4j CircuitBreaker around inter-service calls with fallback responses
refactor_suggestions.benefit: Prevents cascading failures, provides graceful degradation

refactor_suggestions.title: Externalize Tax Rate Configuration
refactor_suggestions.current: Tax rate is hardcoded: subtotal.multiply(new BigDecimal('0.08'))
refactor_suggestions.proposed: Move to application.yml: order.tax-rate: 0.08. Inject via @Value or @ConfigurationProperties
refactor_suggestions.benefit: Configurable per region, no code change needed for tax updates

refactor_suggestions.title: Add Idempotency Key to Order Creation
refactor_suggestions.current: Duplicate order requests could create multiple orders
refactor_suggestions.proposed: Accept idempotency-key header, check if order with same key exists before creating
refactor_suggestions.benefit: Prevents duplicate orders from retry scenarios

detailed_issues.message: Possible NullPointerException in cart item mapping
InterruptedException should not be ignored (in retry logic)
Exception cause should always be preserved
User input is used in URL construction without sanitization
Log statements contain user IDs — potential PII leak in logs
Consider using dedicated DTO classes instead of Map<String, Object>

critical_actions: Refactor createOrder() to reduce cyclomatic complexity below 10
Replace Map<String, Object> with typed DTOs for inter-service communication
Add null checks for all deserialized external data
Implement circuit breaker for inter-service REST calls
positive_aspects: Status transition validation prevents invalid state changes
Null argument validation at method entry points
No hallucinated APIs — all endpoints match OpenAPI specs
